questions.question: Which observation supports the idea that matter is made of tiny particles?
questions.explanation: Sugar dissolves because its tiny particles mix with water particles.

questions.question: Which experiment shows that particles of matter have spaces between them?
questions.explanation: Sugar particles occupy spaces between water particles when dissolved.

questions.question: Which state of matter has particles that vibrate around fixed positions?
questions.explanation: Particles in solids vibrate but remain fixed in position.

questions.question: Why do liquids take the shape of their container?
questions.explanation: Liquid particles can move around each other, allowing liquids to take the shape of their container.

questions.question: Which property of gases allows them to fill an entire container?
questions.explanation: Gas particles move freely and occupy all available space.

questions.question: What is the main reason gases are highly compressible?

options.C: Particles have large spaces between them

questions.explanation: Gases contain large empty spaces between particles.

questions.question: Which activity demonstrates that gas particles move freely?
questions.explanation: Perfume smell spreads because gas particles move freely through air.

questions.question: Which state of matter shows the fastest diffusion?
questions.explanation: Gas particles move faster and are farther apart, so diffusion occurs faster.

questions.question: What happens to particle movement when temperature increases?
questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases the kinetic energy of particles.

questions.question: Which experiment demonstrates the compressibility of gases?
questions.explanation: Air in a syringe can be compressed when the plunger is pushed.

questions.question: Which property explains why solids maintain a fixed shape?
questions.explanation: Strong attraction keeps particles fixed in solids.

questions.question: What type of change occurs when ice melts into water?
questions.explanation: Melting is a physical change where the substance remains the same.

questions.question: Which process converts liquid water into water vapor below boiling point?
questions.explanation: Evaporation occurs at the surface of a liquid below boiling temperature.

questions.question: Why does diffusion occur faster in hot water than in cold water?

options.B: Particles move faster at higher temperatures

questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases particle motion and diffusion rate.

questions.question: Which state of matter has the weakest interparticle forces?
questions.explanation: Gas particles have very weak attractive forces.

questions.question: Which example best represents diffusion in liquids?
questions.explanation: Potassium permanganate color spreads in water due to diffusion.

questions.question: Which state of matter has moderate interparticle attraction?
questions.explanation: Liquids have weaker attraction than solids but stronger than gases.

questions.question: What is the boiling point of water under normal conditions?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100°C under normal atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: Which property explains why liquids flow easily?

options.B: Particles move freely within limited space

questions.explanation: Liquid particles move around each other, allowing flow.

questions.question: Which example demonstrates gas diffusion?
questions.explanation: Smell spreads due to diffusion of gas particles.

questions.question: What happens to particle spacing when matter changes from solid to gas?
questions.explanation: Particles move farther apart when matter changes to gas.

questions.question: Which process changes gas into liquid?
questions.explanation: Condensation is the process of gas turning into liquid.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes particles of liquids?

options.B: Particles are far apart and move freely
options.C: Particles are close but can move around each other

questions.explanation: Liquid particles remain close but can move around each other.

questions.question: Which concept explains why solids do not flow?
questions.explanation: Particles in solids are fixed due to strong attraction.

questions.question: Which property best distinguishes gases from solids?

options.C: Gas particles move freely and are far apart
options.D: Gas particles vibrate in fixed positions

questions.explanation: Gas particles are far apart and move freely unlike solids.
